summary: Recent updates to the media control features in Firefox have significantly improved how the browser manages audio sessions, enhancing the user experience when multiple audio sources are active. The updates introduce a new way to track and manage audio session states, allowing for **more effective handling of audio focus, which means the right audio source plays without confusion when several apps or tabs are open**. Implementations of various algorithms ensure that audio types are correctly identified and managed in real-time, while tests have been added to confirm stability even when audible iframes (like embedded media) appear. This means users can enjoy a smoother audio experience while navigating the web, with less interference from other sources.
